title: [VectorCombine] Fold deinterleave2 with smaller effective element size
author: mshockwave
description:
Found in real-world code where this sequence:
```
%d = llvm.vector.deinterleave2 <vscale x 16 x i32> %v
%f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 0
%f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 1

%low0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 65535)
%low1 = shl <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 16)
%merge0 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %low0, %low1

%high0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 -65536)
%high1 = lshr <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 16)
%merge1 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %high0, %high1
```
is really just doing `deinterleave2` but on `<vscale x 32 x i16>`. That is, the same total vector size but with half the element width. So we can turn it into:
```
%narrow.v = bitcast <vscale x 16 x i32> %v to <vscale x 32 x i16>
%d = llvm.vector.deinterleave2 <vscale x 32 x i16> %narrow.v
%f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 16 x i16>, <vscale x 16 x i16> } %d, 0
%f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 16 x i16>, <vscale x 16 x i16> } %d, 1

%merge0 = bitcast <vscale x 16 x i16> %f0 to <vscale x 8 x i32>
%merge1 = bitcast <vscale x 16 x i16> %f1 to <vscale x 8 x i32>
```
I put this in VectorCombine because I'm worried that the newly created bitcast might be expensive for some targets, hence a profitability check before committing the transformation.

tests.test_body:
define void @narrow_deinterleave2(ptr %p, ptr %out, i32 %evl) {
  %wide.vp.load = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vp.load.nxv16i32.p0(ptr align 4 %p, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  %d = tail call { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } @llvm.vector.deinterleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 16 x i32> %wide.vp.load)
  %f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 0
  %f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 1
  %low0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 65535)
  %low1 = shl <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 16)
  %merge0 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %low0, %low1
  %high0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 -65536)
  %high1 = lshr <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 16)
  %merge1 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %high0, %high1
  %interleaved.vec = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vector.interleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 8 x i32> %merge0, <vscale x 8 x i32> %merge1)
  tail call void @llvm.vp.store.nxv16i32.p0(<vscale x 16 x i32> %interleaved.vec, ptr align 4 %out, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  ret void
}

define void @narrow_deinterleave2_nxv4i64(ptr %p, ptr %out, i32 %evl) {
  %wide.vp.load = tail call <vscale x 4 x i64> @llvm.vp.load.nxv4i64.p0(ptr align 4 %p, <vscale x 4 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  %d = tail call { <vscale x 2 x i64>, <vscale x 2 x i64> } @llvm.vector.deinterleave2.nxv4i64(<vscale x 4 x i64> %wide.vp.load)
  %f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 2 x i64>, <vscale x 2 x i64> } %d, 0
  %f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 2 x i64>, <vscale x 2 x i64> } %d, 1
  %low0 = and <vscale x 2 x i64> %f0, splat (i64 4294967295)
  %low1 = shl <vscale x 2 x i64> %f1, splat (i64 32)
  %merge0 = or disjoint <vscale x 2 x i64> %low0, %low1
  %high0 = and <vscale x 2 x i64> %f1, splat (i64 -4294967296)
  %high1 = lshr <vscale x 2 x i64> %f0, splat (i64 32)
  %merge1 = or disjoint <vscale x 2 x i64> %high0, %high1
  %interleaved.vec = tail call <vscale x 4 x i64> @llvm.vector.interleave2.nxv4i64(<vscale x 2 x i64> %merge0, <vscale x 2 x i64> %merge1)
  tail call void @llvm.vp.store.nxv4i64.p0(<vscale x 4 x i64> %interleaved.vec, ptr align 4 %out, <vscale x 4 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  ret void
}

define void @narrow_deinterleave2_commute(ptr %p, ptr %out, i32 %evl) {
  %wide.vp.load = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vp.load.nxv16i32.p0(ptr align 4 %p, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  %d = tail call { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } @llvm.vector.deinterleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 16 x i32> %wide.vp.load)
  %f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 0
  %f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 1
  %high0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 -65536)
  %high1 = lshr <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 16)
  %merge1 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %high1, %high0
  %low0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 65535)
  %low1 = shl <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 16)
  %merge0 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %low1, %low0
  %interleaved.vec = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vector.interleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 8 x i32> %merge0, <vscale x 8 x i32> %merge1)
  tail call void @llvm.vp.store.nxv16i32.p0(<vscale x 16 x i32> %interleaved.vec, ptr align 4 %out, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  ret void
}

define void @negative_narrow_deinterleave2(ptr %p, ptr %out, i32 %evl) {
  %wide.vp.load = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vp.load.nxv16i32.p0(ptr align 4 %p, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  %d = tail call { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } @llvm.vector.deinterleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 16 x i32> %wide.vp.load)
  %f0 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 0
  %f1 = extractvalue { <vscale x 8 x i32>, <vscale x 8 x i32> } %d, 1
  %high0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 -65536)
  %high1 = lshr <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 16)
  %merge1 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %high1, %high0
  %low0 = and <vscale x 8 x i32> %f0, splat (i32 65535)
  %low1 = shl <vscale x 8 x i32> %f1, splat (i32 16)
  %merge0 = or disjoint <vscale x 8 x i32> %low1, %low0
  %interleaved.vec = tail call <vscale x 16 x i32> @llvm.vector.interleave2.nxv16i32(<vscale x 8 x i32> %merge0, <vscale x 8 x i32> %merge1)
  tail call void @llvm.vp.store.nxv16i32.p0(<vscale x 16 x i32> %interleaved.vec, ptr align 4 %out, <vscale x 16 x i1> splat (i1 true), i32 %evl)
  ret void
}

comments.author: mshockwave
comments.body: We also need to add the `shufflevector` (on fixed vector) equivalent of this pattern.
